topics: Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter (solid, liquid, gas); change of state
subtopics: Absorption of heat in melting; Cooling by evaporation

topics: Elements, compounds and mixtures. Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures, colloids and suspensions; Physical and chemical changes
Atoms and molecules; Law of Chemical Combination; Chemical formulae; Atomic and molecular masses
Sub-atomic particles (Electrons, protons, neutrons); Models of atom; Valency; Atomic Number and Mass Number; Isotopes and Isobars

Prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; multicellular organisms; Cell membrane and cell wall; cell organelles (chloroplast, mitochondria, vacuoles, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus); nucleus; chromosomes
subtopics: Basic structure and number of chromosomes

topics: Structure and functions of animal tissues (four types) and plant tissues (Meristematic and Permanent); Organs, Organ System, Organism

chapters.chapter_name: Unit II: Organization in the Living World
chapters.source_heading: Unit II: Organization in the Living World

topics: Distance, displacement, velocity; uniform and non-uniform motion; acceleration; distance-time and velocity-time graphs; elementary idea of uniform circular motion
Newton's Laws of Motion; Action and Reaction forces; Inertia (of a body and mass); Momentum; Force and Acceleration
Universal Law of Gravitation; Gravity; Acceleration due to Gravity; Mass and Weight; Free fall
Thrust and Pressure; Archimedes' Principle; Buoyancy
Work done by a Force; Energy, power; Kinetic and Potential energy; Conservation of energy (excluding commercial unit)
Nature of sound; propagation in various media; speed of sound; range of hearing; ultrasound; reflection of sound; echo

chapters.chapter_name: Unit III: Motion, Force and Work
chapters.source_heading: Unit III: Motion, Force and Work

topics: Types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation; endothermic/exothermic; oxidation/reduction

Definitions (H+ and OH- ions); indicators; neutralization; pH scale; importance of pH in everyday life; preparation/uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching powder, Baking soda, Washing soda, Plaster of Paris
subtopics: Definition relating to logarithm not required for pH
Halogen, alcohol, ketone, aldehyde functional groups

topics: Properties of metals/non-metals; Reactivity series; Ionic compounds; Metallurgical processes; Corrosion and prevention

Covalent bonds; Versatile nature of carbon; Hydrocarbons (saturated/unsaturated); Homologous series; Nomenclature (alkanes, alkenes, alkynes) with functional groups; Chemical properties (combustion, oxidation, addition, substitution)
Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (properties and uses only); soaps and detergents

chapters.chapter_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
chapters.source_heading: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour

topics: Nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals

chapters.chapter_name: Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
chapters.source_heading: Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
topics: Tropic movements; plant hormones; Nervous system (Voluntary, involuntary, reflex); Chemical co-ordination (animal hormones)
Reproduction in animals/plants (asexual/sexual); reproductive health; family planning methods; Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS; Child bearing and women's health
Heredity; Mendel's Laws of inheritance; Sex determination
subtopics: Brief introduction to evolution concepts implied in heredity section

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour -> Chemical Reactions and Equations
Unit II: World of Living -> Life processes
Unit III: Natural Phenomena -> Natural Phenomena
Unit IV: Effects of Current -> Effects of Current
Unit V: Natural Resources -> Natural Resources

practicals.title: Preparation of solutions (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 1. Preparation of: a) true solution of common salt, sugar and alum b) suspension of soil, chalk powder and fine sand c) colloidal solution of starch in water and egg albumin/milk

practicals.title: Distinguishing mixture vs compound (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 2. Preparation of a) A mixture b) A compound using iron filings and sulphur powder and distinguishing between these

practicals.title: Physical or chemical changes reactions (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 3. Perform the following reactions: Iron with copper sulphate; Burning of magnesium ribbon; Zinc with dilute sulphuric acid; Heating of copper sulphate crystals; Sodium sulphate with barium chloride

practicals.title: Stained temporary mounts (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 4. Preparation of stained temporary mounts of onion peel, human cheek cells & to record observations and draw their labeled diagrams

practicals.title: Identification of tissues (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 5. Identification of Parenchyma, Collenchyma and Sclerenchyma tissues in plants; striped, smooth and cardiac muscle fibers and nerve cells

practicals.title: Melting point of ice and boiling point of water (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 6. Determination of the melting point of ice and the boiling point of water.

practicals.title: Laws of reflection of sound (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 7. Verification of the laws of reflection of sound.

practicals.title: Density of solid (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 8. Determination of the density of solid (denser than water) by using a spring balance and a measuring cylinder.

practicals.title: Archimedes' Principle verification (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 9. Establishing the relation between the loss in weight of a solid when fully immersed in Tap water / Strongly salty water with the weight of water displaced

practicals.title: Speed of pulse (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 10. Determination of the speed of a pulse propagated through a stretched string/slinky.

practicals.title: Conservation of mass (Class IX)
practicals.source_text: 11. Verification of the law of conservation of mass in a chemical reaction.

practicals.title: Finding pH (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 1A. Finding the pH of Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, NaOH solution, Ethanoic Acid, Lemon juice, Water, Hydrogen Carbonate solution

practicals.title: Properties of acids and bases (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 1B. Studying properties on reaction with Litmus, Zinc metal, Solid sodium carbonate

practicals.title: Types of reactions (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 2. Performing: Action of water on quicklime; Heat on ferrous sulphate crystals; Iron nails in copper sulphate; Sodium sulphate with barium chloride

practicals.title: Reactivity series (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 3. Observing action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al on salt solutions; Arranging metals in decreasing order of reactivity

practicals.title: Ohm's Law verification (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 4. Studying dependence of potential difference across a resistor on current; plotting V-I graph

practicals.title: Equivalent resistance (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 5. Determination of equivalent resistance of two resistors in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Stomata mount (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 6. Preparing a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: CO2 in respiration (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 7. Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Properties of acetic acid (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 8. Study properties: Odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate

practicals.title: Soap cleaning capacity (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 9. Study of comparative cleaning capacity of soap in soft and hard water.

practicals.title: Focal length determination (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 10. Determination of focal length of Concave mirror and Convex lens by obtaining image of distant object.

practicals.title: Rectangular glass slab (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 11. Tracing path of ray through rectangular glass slab; measuring angles of incidence, refraction and emergence.

practicals.title: Binary fission and Budding (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 12. Studying binary fission in Amoeba, budding in yeast and Hydra with prepared slides.

practicals.title: Prism ray tracing (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 13. Tracing path of rays through a glass prism.

practicals.title: Embryo parts identification (Class X)
practicals.source_text: 14. Identification of different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed.

projects.title: Portfolio (Class IX/X)
projects.source_text: For Internal Assessment: Portfolio that includes classwork and other sample of student's work.

projects.title: Projects/Posters/Presentations
projects.source_text: Diverse methods of assessment... may include - short tests, oral test, quiz, concept maps, projects, posters, presentations and enquiry based scientific investigations etc.

marks_distribution: Class IX: Unit I (25), Unit II (22), Unit III (27), Unit IV (6). Total Theory: 80. Internal Assessment: 20.
Class X: Unit I (25), Unit II (25), Unit III (12), Unit IV (13), Unit V (5). Total Theory: 80. Internal Assessment: 20.
Internal Assessment Breakdown: Periodic Tests (Best of two) - 5 marks; Diverse Methods/Projects/Presentations - 5 marks; Practical/Laboratory Work - 5 marks; Portfolio - 5 marks.
excluded_or_non_assessed_content: Separating the components of a mixture
Definition relating to logarithm for pH scale (Class X)
Derivation of Mirror formula and Lens formula (Class X)
Commercial unit of Energy in Work, Energy and Power section (Class IX)
Colour of the sun at sunrise and sunset (Scattering topic Class X)
Information presented in NCERT text book boxes
teacher_notes: The present syllabus has been designed around seven broad themes: Food; Materials; The World of the Living; How Things Work; Moving Things, People and Ideas; Natural Phenomenon and Natural Resources.
Curricular Goals are interdependent, not separate curricular pieces of study. (Reference: National Curriculum Framework for School Education – 2023).

The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination.
Practicals should be conducted alongside the concepts taught in theory classes.
All practical work listed in the syllabus must be completed and maintained as a record by students.
